cross_references.finding: Onboarding time remains above target despite improvement efforts
Two critical bugs in payment module prioritized and patched
Sales department over budget in both EMEA and NAMER regions due to pipeline growth
Sprint 12 velocity drop linked to two engineers on leave
SSO delivery delayed to Q2 due to security review dependency

narrative.executive_summary: In Q1 2026, we maintained a stable engineering velocity averaging 48 points per sprint, successfully shipping the Salesforce integration on schedule and patching two critical payment module bugs. However, key strategic objectives remain at risk, notably the goal to reduce customer onboarding time by 40%, which currently stands at 9.5 days, above the 8-day target. The SSO delivery has been delayed to Q2 due to a necessary security review, impacting our integration timeline. Sales departments in both EMEA and NAMER exceeded their budgets by approximately 8%, driven by pipeline growth, though overall spending remains under budget by 1.44%. Engineering capacity is fully allocated, with no new projects approved until March, ensuring focus on current priorities.
key_accomplishments: Stable average sprint velocity of 48 points maintained
Two critical payment module bugs identified and patched
Onboarding improvement plan delivered, reducing time from 11 to 9.5 days
Completion of SSO security review enabling build start in Q2

risks.risk: Customer onboarding time remains above target, risking customer satisfaction and retention
risks.mitigation: Implement and monitor onboarding improvement plan with targeted interventions and customer feedback

risks.risk: SSO delivery delayed to Q2 due to security review dependency, impacting integration roadmap
risks.mitigation: Prioritize SSO build in Q2 Sprint 1 and closely monitor progress to avoid further delays

risks.risk: Sales departments in EMEA and NAMER over budget by ~8%, potentially impacting financial targets
risks.mitigation: Review sales spend and pipeline growth alignment; adjust forecasts and controls as needed

narrative.budget_headline: Overall budget remains under by 1.44%, despite sales departments in EMEA and NAMER exceeding budgets by approximately 8% due to pipeline-driven spend.

recommendation.rationale: Current strategic initiatives demonstrate solid progress with manageable risks. Focused efforts on onboarding improvements and SSO delivery are critical. Budget overruns in sales are justified by pipeline growth and do not threaten overall financial health.

next_quarter_focus: Execute SSO build and delivery starting Q2 Sprint 1
Drive onboarding time reduction to meet 8-day target
Monitor and control sales department spending relative to pipeline growth
Complete Q1 engineering retrospective and pipeline reporting
Maintain focus on critical bug resolution and system stability
